evidence_excerpt: After diagnosis and treatment adjustments, PET-CT showed reduced lymph node uptake and spirometry normalized, but a non-productive cough persisted; daclizumab was permanently discontinued.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: All systemic symptoms resolved except ongoing non-productive cough, which did not improve with inhaled or short-course oral corticosteroids.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Inhaled corticosteroids were commenced after clinical diagnosis of sarcoidosis.
A short trial of oral corticosteroids followed, with no effect on the cough.
PET-CT 6 months after cessation of daclizumab showed reduction in lymph node uptake.
Repeat spirometry 10 weeks after stopping corticosteroids was normal (FEV1/FVC 80.5%).
Based on improvement, daclizumab was deemed the probable cause and permanently discontinued.

notes_on_uncertainty: Follow-up beyond approximately 6 months is not provided; persistence or resolution of cough after the mid-term period is unclear. Timing of some events is anchored to daclizumab cessation but mapped approximately to the diagnosis date per instructions.
